Standard prescription practice; 3 refills is a common number for chronic maintenance medications allowing 90+ days of therapy

https://data.world/rhoyt/librehealth-educational-ehr and https://forums.librehealth.io/t/nhanes-data-in-libreehr/735; setup_librehealth.sh script; verified via OpenHealth News article on LibreHealth educational use

privileged_info_summary: The task involves writing a prescription in LibreHealth EHR for Metformin 500mg tablets. All prescription parameters are verified as medically sound: Metformin is a real antidiabetic drug available in 500mg tablets, the dosage of one tablet twice daily with meals is a standard starting dose, a quantity of 60 tablets represents a standard 30-day supply for this dosing regimen, and 3 refills is consistent with chronic medication prescribing practices. The task uses real patient data from the National Health and Nutrition Examination Survey (NHANES) 2011-2012, although the exact patient count is 9,600 rather than the claimed 9,375.
